circular.title: Master Circular - Income Recognition, Asset Classification, Provisioning and Other Related Matters - UCBs
circular.summary: This Master Circular consolidates the prudential framework for Urban Co-operative Banks concerning the identification and management of Non-Performing Assets. It mandates an objective and uniform system for income recognition, asset classification, and provisioning based on record of recovery. Key features include the definition of 'out of order' for overdrafts, specific timelines for classifying incipient stress through Special Mention Accounts, and staggered provisioning requirements for standard assets.

clauses.text: Reserve Bank has introduced prudential norms for income recognition, asset classification and provisioning for the advances portfolio.
Income recognition should be objective and based on record of recovery.
The requirements of the State Co-operative Societies Acts and/or rules must be followed if they are more stringent than these norms.
clauses.summary: Compliance with State Co-operative Societies Acts

clauses.text: A loan or advance is an NPA where: (i) Interest and/or installment of principal remain overdue for more than 90 days in respect of a Term Loan. (ii) The account remains 'out of order' for 90 days in respect of an Overdraft/Cash Credit. (iii) The bill remains overdue for more than 90 days in the case of bills purchased and discounted. (iv) Agricultural advances: Overdue for two crop seasons (short duration) or one crop season (long duration). (v) Any amount to be received remains overdue for more than 90 days in respect of other accounts.
A credit card account is treated as NPA if the minimum amount due is not paid fully within 90 days from the payment due date.
Identification of NPAs must be done on an on-going basis and provisions made at the end of each calendar quarter.
All UCBs shall classify loans as SMA: - SMA-0: 0-30 days overdue. - SMA-1: 31-60 days overdue. - SMA-2: 61-90 days overdue.
Classification of NPAs must be done borrower-wise and not facility-wise.

Credit facilities backed by Central Government guarantees though overdue should not be treated as NPA for provisioning (except for income recognition), but State Government guarantees attract normal norms if overdue >90 days.
Accounts classified as 'standard assets' should be immediately re-classified as 'sub-standard assets' upon restructuring.
Banks must provide for the diminution in the fair value of restructured advances.
Total provisions required (normal + diminution) are capped at 100% of the outstanding debt.
Banks should classify assets into: Standard, Sub-standard, Doubtful, and Loss.
Accounts with potential threats to recovery (erosion in security value >50%) should be straightaway classified as Doubtful or Loss assets.
Income from NPA is not recognized on accrual basis but booked only when actually received.
If an advance becomes NPA, interest accrued and credited to income account in the current and past periods, if uncollected, must be reversed.

Provisioning requirements: (i) Loss Assets: 100% should be provided for or written off. (ii) Doubtful Assets: 100% for the unsecured portion. For the secured portion: - Up to 1 year: 20% - 1 to 3 years: 30% - More than 3 years: 100% (effective April 1, 2010). (iii) Sub-standard Assets: General provision of 10% on total outstanding. (iv) Standard Assets: - Agriculture/SME: 0.25% - CRE: 1.00% - CRE-RH: 0.75% - Others: 0.40%
The entire amount due to the bank must be provided for over a period not exceeding four quarters commencing with the quarter in which the fraud was detected.
If there is a delay in reporting the fraud to RBI, the entire provisioning is required to be made at once.

obligations.text: Classify loans as NPA if interest or principal installment remains overdue for >90 days.
obligations.plain_english: Classify loans as non-performing if payments are overdue for more than 90 days.

obligations.text: Implement system-based asset classification in CBS.
obligations.plain_english: Use a system-based approach to classify assets in the Core Banking System.

obligations.text: Classify stressed accounts into SMA-0 (0-30 days), SMA-1 (31-60 days), and SMA-2 (61-90 days).
obligations.plain_english: Classify stressed accounts into Special Mention Accounts based on the number of days overdue.

obligations.text: Reverse interest/fees accrued but not realized when an account turns NPA.
obligations.plain_english: Reverse any interest or fees that have been accrued but not realized when an account becomes non-performing.
Maintain provisions for standard assets based on the type of asset.

obligations.text: Charge 10% general provision on Sub-standard assets and 20%-100% on Doubtful assets.
obligations.plain_english: Charge provisions on sub-standard and doubtful assets based on their classification.

obligations.text: Provide for 100% of the entire amount due for fraud accounts over max 4 quarters.
obligations.plain_english: Provide for the entire amount due for fraud accounts over a maximum of 4 quarters.

obligations.text: Report annual NPA figures to the Regional Office of the RBI.
obligations.plain_english: Report annual non-performing asset figures to the Regional Office of the Reserve Bank of India.

obligations.text: Submit CRILC reports for borrowers with aggregate exposure ≥₹5 crore.
obligations.plain_english: Submit Credit Repository of Information on Large Credits reports for borrowers with aggregate exposure of ₹5 crore or more.

prohibitions.text: Do not treat credit facilities backed by Central Government guarantees as NPA for provisioning (except for income recognition).
prohibitions.plain_english: Do not treat credit facilities backed by Central Government guarantees as non-performing for provisioning purposes, except for income recognition.

prohibitions.text: Do not delay in reporting fraud to RBI.
prohibitions.plain_english: Do not delay in reporting fraud to the Reserve Bank of India.

penalties.condition: Non-compliance with system-based asset classification in CBS.
Non-submission of CRILC reports for borrowers with aggregate exposure ≥₹5 crore.
Non-provisioning for fraud accounts over max 4 quarters.

definitions.definition: A loan or an advance where interest and/or installment of principal remain overdue for a period of more than 90 days.
definitions.plain_english: A loan or advance that is overdue for more than 90 days.

definitions.definition: An OD/CC account where the balance remains continuously in excess of the limit for 90 days, or where there are no/insufficient credits to cover interest for 90 days.
definitions.plain_english: An overdraft or cash credit account that is overdue for 90 days.

definitions.definition: Any amount due to the bank under any credit facility if not paid by the due date.
definitions.plain_english: An amount that is due but not paid by the due date.

definitions.definition: An asset which does not disclose any problems and does not carry more than normal risk.
definitions.plain_english: An asset that is not problematic and carries normal risk.

definitions.definition: An asset which has remained NPA for a period less than or equal to 12 months.
definitions.plain_english: An asset that has been non-performing for less than or equal to 12 months.

definitions.definition: An asset which has remained in the sub-standard category for a period of 12 months.
definitions.plain_english: An asset that has been sub-standard for 12 months.

definitions.definition: An asset where loss has been identified by the bank/auditors/RBI but the amount has not been written off.
definitions.plain_english: An asset where a loss has been identified but not written off.

definitions.definition: A period of one year from the date when the first payment of interest or installment of principal falls due under the terms of the restructuring package.
definitions.plain_english: A one-year period from the first payment due date under a restructuring package.

references.circular_number: Master Directions on Fraud Risk Management
